thehindu.com.name: The Hindu
thehindu.com.notes: Known for traditional, sober reporting; often critical of government policy.

ndtv.com.name: NDTV
ndtv.com.notes: Shifted from Center-Left to Center/Center-Right after Adani acquisition.

republicworld.com.name: Republic TV
republicworld.com.notes: Known for high-decibel, nationalist framing; strongly pro-government.

indianexpress.com.name: The Indian Express
indianexpress.com.notes: Strong focus on investigative journalism; maintains a neutral but critical stance.

hindustantimes.com.name: Hindustan Times
hindustantimes.com.notes: One of India's oldest English dailies; generally follows a pragmatic approach.

deccanherald.com.name: Deccan Herald
deccanherald.com.notes: Major Southern daily known for independence.

news18.com.name: News18
news18.com.notes: Part of Mukesh Ambani's media conglomerate.

thewire.in.name: The Wire
thewire.in.notes: Aggressively critical of the establishment; focuses on civil liberties.

theprint.in.name: The Print
theprint.in.notes: Focuses on policy and strategic affairs; often features diverse op-eds.

thequint.com.name: The Quint
thequint.com.notes: Digital-first outlet with a strong focus on social justice and political critique.

outlookindia.com.name: Outlook India
outlookindia.com.notes: Known for deep-dive features and investigative cover stories.

scroll.in.name: Scroll.in
scroll.in.notes: Strong focus on social issues and cultural reporting.

swarajyamag.com.name: Swarajya
swarajyamag.com.notes: Self-described as liberal center-right; focuses on Indic civilization and economics.

opindia.com.name: OpIndia
opindia.com.notes: Digital-first outlet; often focuses on fact-checking mainstream media from a right-wing lens.

indiatimes.com.name: The Times of India / Navbharat Times
indiatimes.com.ownership: Bennett, Coleman and Co. Ltd (Jain Family)
indiatimes.com.notes: World's largest-circulated English daily; often pragmatic and pro-business.

jagran.com.name: Dainik Jagran
jagran.com.notes: India's largest-read daily; known for a conservative, nationalist stance.

bhaskar.com.name: Dainik Bhaskar
bhaskar.com.notes: Largest newspaper group in India by circulation; often independent in its regional reporting.

sakal.com.name: Sakal
sakal.com.notes: Major Marathi daily with deep regional influence.

lokmat.com.name: Lokmat
lokmat.com.notes: Leading Marathi newspaper with vast rural reach.

anandabazar.com.name: Anandabazar Patrika
anandabazar.com.notes: Dominant Bengali daily; historical center of Bengali journalism.

mathrubhumi.com.name: Mathrubhumi
mathrubhumi.com.ownership: The Mathrubhumi Printing & Publishing Co. Ltd
mathrubhumi.com.notes: Major Malayalam daily with a strong legacy of independence.

prajavani.net.name: Prajavani
prajavani.net.notes: Leading Kannada newspaper known for its progressive stance.

eenadu.net.name: Eenadu
eenadu.net.notes: Largest circulated Telugu newspaper; influential in regional politics.

google.com.name: Google News
google.com.notes: Aggregator that surface stories from multiple publishers.

aninews.in.name: ANI (Asian News International)
aninews.in.notes: India's leading multimedia news agency; often criticized for a pro-government stance.

ptinews.com.name: PTI (Press Trust of India)
ptinews.com.notes: India's largest news agency; maintained a reputation for neutral, factual wire reporting.

zeenews.india.com.name: Zee News
zeenews.india.com.notes: Historically pro-nationalist and often aligned with the ruling party's narratives.
